content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
steven R. Crooks 
Hefner, Stark & Marois 
October 3, 1989 
2710 Gateway Oaks Dr., Ste. 300 South 
Sacramento, CA 
95833-3501 
Dear Mr. Crooks: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-464 
You have requested advice on behalf of the political action 
committee of the Building Industry Association, BAC-PAC, 
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act. 1 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
When disclosing member contributions received through 
the collection of association dues, has BAC-PAC received the 
contributions when the members' dues are received by the Building 
Industry Association (the "Association") or when the Association 
transmits the funds to BAC-PAC? 
2. 
If the date of receipt'by BAC-PAC is the date the 
Association receives the members' dues, what are the Association's 
recordkeeping responsibilities with regard to such contributions? 
3. 
Is there a requirement that the Association transmit 
member contributions at any particular times during the year? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. 
BAC-PAC receives the members' contributions when the 
dues payments are received by the Association. 
2. 
The Association must report the contributions to BAC-PAC 
no later than the closing date of the campaign statement on which 
the contributions must be disclosed by the committee. 
Therefore, 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 81000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


steven R. Crooks 
Page 2 
the Association must keep whatever records are reasonably 
necessary for BAC-PAC to comply with its disclosure obligations. 
3. 
The Act does not specify when the Association must 
transmit member contributions to BAC-PAC. 
FACTS 
BAC-PAC is the sponsored committee of the Building Industry 
Association. 
contributions to BAC-PAC are collected as a portion 
of dues paid to the Association by its members. 
During our 
telephone conversation of September 8, 1989, you indicated that 
the members have earmarked a specified portion of their dues to be 
contributed to the committee. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires committees established pursuant to section 
82013(a) ("recipient committees") to file periodic reports 
disclosing, among other things, contributions received. 
(Sections 
84200-84221.) 
The recordkeeping section of the 1989 Information 
Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform 
Act states: 
A contribution is "received" the day the candidate 
or committee treasurer or his or her agent actually 
receives the contribution, not when the contribution 
is deposited into the committee's bank account. 
Information Manual, p. 52. 
(Emphasis added.) 
Because the Association's members have earmarked a specified 
portion of their dues as contributions to BAC-PAC, the committee 
"receives" the contributions on the date the Association receives 
the dues from the members. 
With regard to recordkeeping responsibilities, section 84306 
provides: 
... AII contributions received by a person acting as 
an agent of a committee shall be reported promptly to 
the committee's treasurer or any of his or her desig-
nated agents. 
"Promptly" as used in this section 
means not later than the closing date of any campaign 
statement the committee or candidate for whom the 
contribution is intended is required to file. 
As the agent of the committee for the receipt of contributions, 
the Association must report to BAC-PAC the contributions it 
collects. 
In order to provide the committee with adequate 
information to fulfill its filing obligations, the Association 
should record and provide to the committee the name, address, 


steven R. Crooks 
Page 3 
occupation and employer of each person who has earmarked $25 or 
more in contributions during the calendar year. 
Finally, as noted in the conclusions stated above, the Act 
does not specify when contributions collected by an agent of a 
committee must actually be transmitted to the committee. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional 
questions .. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Dovovan 
General Counsel

P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804

Rt::. 
Re: 
BAC-PAC, Sponsored committee of the Building 
Industry Association 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
27l!l Gateway Oaks Drive 
Suite .100 South 
Sacramento, CA 95R.1J~JSIIJ 
(916) 925-6620 
Fax II 925-1127 
Bay A rea Office 
(415) 8.17-2111 
This letter is a request for formal written advice 
from the Commission pursuant to Government Code § 83114(b) 
and Commission Regulation 18329. 
I 
am writing as the 
authorized 
representative 
for 
the political action 
committee of the Building Industry Association, BAC-PAC. 
BAC-PAC is a 
sponsored recipient committee of the BIA, 
which receives most of its contributions from a percentage 
of members' dues received by BIA. 
BIA then makes transfers 
of that percentage of members' dues which are earmarked as 
contributions, to the PAC on a periodic basis. 
My question, on behalf of BAC-PAC and BIA, is whether 
the member contributions to BAC-PAC for members' dues are 
deemed recei ved by BAC-PAC 
when the members' 
dues are 
received 
by 
BIA 
or 
when 
BIA 
transfers 
the 
actual 
contribution 
amounts 
to 
BAC-PAC? 
Also, 
if the 
contributions are deemed received by 
BAC-PAC 
when the 
members' 
dues are received by BIA, 
what recordkeeping 
responsibilities does BIA have under the Political Reform 
Act concerning such contributions, 
for 
example, 
under 
Government Code § 84104 and Commission Regulation 18401. 
Finally, as a general question, do any provisions of the 
Political Reform Act or any of the Commission regulations 
(especially the new statutes and regulations promulgated 
under Proposition 73), have any impact on the timing of 
when BIA physically transfers the members' contributions to 
BAC-PAC, that is, is it preferable or required that such 
transfers be made at any particular times during a calendar 
or fiscal year? 


HEFNER 
STARK & 
M .. 4.ROIS 
lAw Offices 
Jeanne Pritchard 
August 7, 1989 
Page 2 
Thank 
you 
for 
your 
assistance 
with this 
advice 
request. 
Please do not hesitate to contact me if you need 
additional information to consider these issues. 
very truly yours, 
HEFNERjt?TARK & ~OIA 
By <~ "{lJ~f[r 
steven R. Crooks

Dwight Hansen

My question, on behalf of BAC-PAC and BIA, is whether

when the members' 
dues are

under 
Government Code

84104 and Commission Regulation 18401. 
Finally, as a general question, do any provisions of the 
Political Reform Act or any of the Commission regulations 
(especially the new statutes and regulations promulgated 
under Proposition 73), have any impact on the timing of 
when BIA physically transfers the members' contributions to 
BAC-PAC, that is, is it preferable or required that such 
transfers be made at any particular times during a calendar 
or fiscal year?

you 
for

request.

STARK & MAROIS

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
steven R. Crooks 
Hefner, Stark & Marois 
August 10, 1989 
2710 Gateway Oaks Dr., Ste. 300 So. 
Sacramento, CA 
95833-3501 
Re: 
Letter No. 49-464 
Dear Mr. Crooks: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on August 9, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660
